proposals.source: /tmp/src_2024ND29.txt (PDF via pdftotext -layout)
proposals.evidence: Paragraph 25 contains a long quoted passage from United States v. Hyde. The DB text has fused two parts of a sentence that should read 'and inform the defendant of, and determine that the defendant understands,' but appears in DB as 'and inform the defendant of, and the defendant understands,' with the word 'determine that the' omitted, and 'determine' misplaced later as 'numerous determine consequences' instead of 'numerous consequences'. The source PDF shows correct spacing and word order across line breaks (337-338: 'and\n      determine that the defendant understands,'). The text must be reconstructed to restore the missing phrase.
proposals.note: This error occurs in the long U.S. Supreme Court quote spanning lines 326-360 in the source. The error involves both word omission ('determine that') and text fusion with an incorrectly positioned word ('determine'). The curly quote character (U+2019) is preserved in both versions.

flags.description: Missing form-feed byte (0x0C) between page break and running header. The PDF source contains a form-feed at byte position 1170 after 'appellant.' which marks the page break. The DB text lacks this byte, causing the page 2 running header 'Stancel v. Stancel, et al.' to be improperly fused with preceding text without a space separator between 'DAKOTA' and 'Stancel'. This is a structural artifact that should be handled by automated page furniture processing, not a content error.

proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND4.pdf lines 1-5 (top header), lines 30-38 (lawyer information through opinion start)
proposals.evidence: The PDF places the 'FILED IN THE OFFICE OF THE CLERK OF SUPREME COURT JANUARY 10, 2024 STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA' text only at the very top of the document as a filing header (lines 1-5). The DB has mistakenly inserted this same text after the attorney information (Kiara C. Kraus-Parr) and before 'State v. Gonzalez'. This misplaced text does not appear in the PDF at that location. The correct structure per PDF: attorney info → blank line → 'State v. Gonzalez' running header → opinion body.
proposals.note: This is a clear ingestion artifact. The filing notice was extracted from the PDF header and incorrectly placed mid-document. Removing it restores the correct document flow.

proposals.old_exact:
[¶13] We reverse and remand for resentencing consistent with this opinion.

IV
proposals.new_exact:
IV

[¶13] We reverse and remand for resentencing consistent with this opinion.
proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND4.pdf lines 198-200: 'IV' appears on line 198, then blank, then '[¶13]' on line 200
proposals.evidence: In the PDF (authoritative source), the Roman numeral section heading 'IV' appears BEFORE paragraph [¶13]. The DB has reversed this order: [¶13] appears first, followed by 'IV'. This violates the document structure since section headings should precede the paragraphs they introduce. The opinion has four sections (I, II, III, IV); section IV contains [¶13] and [¶14], so IV must come before [¶13].
proposals.note: The section heading introduces the concluding remarks. The missequence in the DB breaks the logical document hierarchy. After the fix, section IV properly introduces [¶13] 'We reverse and remand...' followed by [¶14] signature block.
